content:
Character: Maya Delgado
Perception: Maya has this warm, grounded presence — olive skin, paint under her nails, eyes that study you like she's already composing you on canvas. She's quiet but not shy; there's a confidence in how she observes. We share rooftop evenings — I play, she sketches — and it's one of my favorite rituals in this building. She's seriously talented; her lines capture something photographs miss. I think she's tougher than she looks, carrying family weight she doesn't talk about. I see her as a creative companion, someone who understands the loneliness of making art. I know she works at the coffee shop and sends money home. I sense she's painting us all without asking permission.
summary:
Character: Maya Delgado
Perception: Maya has this warm, grounded presence — olive skin, paint under her nails, eyes that study you like she's already composing you on canvas. She's quiet but not shy; there's a confidence in how she observes. We share rooftop evenings — I play, she sketches — and it's one of my favorite rituals in this building. She's seriously talented; her lines capture something photographs miss. I think she's tougher than she looks, carrying family weight she doesn't talk about. I see her as a creative companion, someone who understands the loneliness of making art. I know she works at the coffee shop and sends money home. I sense she's painting us all without asking permission.
